Parinay, could you please update the patch to fix the code conflict? Thanks.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

(4 comments)

Besides the comments I posted on conf-sanity.sh test_88, I also found that test case always failed on ZFS, due to "tunefs.lustre FATAL: Device lustre-mdt1/mdt1 has not been formatted with mkfs.lustre".

Please improve the patch per these comments, and fix code conflicts and style issue. Thanks.

> Parinay, could you please update the patch to fix the code

Patch Set 10:

Just one question: if "-e" option is specified firstly, then it means that all old parameters should be erased, you set the "ldd_params[0] = '\0'" to indicate that. After that, if "-p" option is specified, then the new parameter will make the "ldd_params[0]" as other value. So the on disk old parameters for ZFS backend will not be erased, right? I am not sure whether I missed something.

parents.subject: LU-8900 doc: Lustre snapshot man page

commit.subject: LU-7131 utils: add "--erase-param" option to tunefs.lustre
commit.message:
LU-7131 utils: add "--erase-param" option to tunefs.lustre

This patch is to fix the following issues:

- A new option "--erase-param" is added to tunefs.lustre, which allows
to remove all instances of a specific parameter.

- The behavior of option "--param" is improved a little. It will erase
all previously stored instances of the parameter, and then store all
new values specified in the command line.

- For zfs only, the patch introduces a "null value" rule in the form
of "<key>=" to mark a property to be removed from dataset by
zfs_write_ldd() finally.

- To keep the right semantics, if "--erase-params" is specified, all
old parameters should be erased before any new changes are done.

- test_89 conf-sanity.sh is added to verify these new features.
